rationale: The watchdog needs to distinguish 'agent exited 0 (clean, work likely done)' vs 'agent exited non-zero (crash)' when deciding whether to auto-heal by running `ta draft build`. Storing exit_code on the state avoids re-reading the process table after the fact.
alternatives: Add separate field on GoalRun (redundant with state)

decision: Default `ta goal list` shows Failed+staging goals rather than requiring `--all`
rationale: The core problem in TRACE-1 is discoverability: users don't know to run `--all` when their goal disappears. Showing recoverable-failed by default means the common case (watchdog-killed goal with complete agent work) is immediately visible. Truly terminal failed goals (no staging) remain hidden to avoid clutter.
alternatives: Always show Failed in default list (too noisy)
Add --recoverable flag (still requires knowing to add it)
Show footer hint without listing (insufficient — user needs to see the goal tag to act)

decision: Progress journal uses GOAL_ID_PLACEHOLDER substitution in run.rs rather than positional format arg
rationale: The CLAUDE.md injection string uses `format!()` with many named fields; inserting `{goal_id}` as a positional arg would require reordering the entire format call. String substitution of a sentinel value (`GOAL_ID_PLACEHOLDER`) after format!() is simpler and avoids touching the existing arg layout.
alternatives: Named format arg `{goal_id}` (requires restructuring format! call)

decision: Watchdog writes audit entry via GoalAuditLedger rather than direct file append
rationale: GoalAuditLedger provides hash-chaining and consistent serialization. Bypassing it with a direct write would produce audit entries incompatible with chain verification.
alternatives: Emit through event bus (adds latency, may lose entries on crash)

decision: TRACE-1/TRACE-2 checks placed in verify_constitution() (ta goal check), not a separate ta audit command
rationale: These are workspace integrity invariants that fit naturally alongside the existing constitution checks. Users already know to run `ta goal check` for health checks; adding a separate command would fragment the verification surface. They can be extracted to a dedicated `ta audit integrity` command later if the check surface grows.
alternatives: New `ta goal integrity` subcommand (separate discovery surface)
`ta audit check` (audit crate doesn't exist yet for CLI dispatch)

decision: Remote template install downloads archive and guides manual extraction rather than using tar/flate2
rationale: The workspace has no tar/flate2 crate. Adding them would pull in C dependencies (libz). For v0.14.8, local path install is fully automated; remote downloads save the archive and print clear extraction steps. Future phase can add a pure-Rust extractor.
alternatives: Add tar + flate2 workspace deps (C deps)

decision: plan_wizard uses stdin readline without an agent call
rationale: The plan item says no agent call is needed. stdin readline is simpler, faster, works offline, and requires no staging workspace. The output is structurally correct PLAN.md immediately.
alternatives: Call an agent to structure the input
Use a TUI library for the wizard

decision: Captured daemon_config fields before move to fix borrow-after-move in web.rs
rationale: build_full_router takes ownership of daemon_config. The web_ui flag and bind info need to be used after the move for the startup log message. Extracting the values (web_ui_enabled, web_ui_port, web_ui_bind) before the move is the minimal correct fix with no cloning of the entire config.
